- This suggests a potentially resectable pancreatic tumor, pending further staging.

- Residual disease status is R1, indicating microscopic residual tumor after resection.

- The absence of distant metastasis (M0) is favorable but the R1 resection and nodal involvement increase risk of recurrence.

- Imaging does not demonstrate gross vascular invasion or distant metastasis on this slice.
- Clinical and pathologic data indicate advanced local disease with nodal involvement and microscopic residual tumor.
- The patient has documented tumor recurrence within 211 days post-diagnosis.
- From an abdominal radiologist’s perspective, this case represents an aggressive pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with poor prognosis despite initial surgical intervention.
- Close imaging follow-up and multidisciplinary management are essential.
